rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for interface behavior and implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings.
Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Ensure all identifiers in AGREE annexes are visible in the target component scope.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope: type for interface behavior, implementation for binding.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should appear after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Component type contracts should relate visible outputs to visible inputs.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for implementation-level bindings only.
Do not reference event ports as numeric or Boolean values without explicit data representation.
Do not introduce identifiers not present in the current component's visible scope.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
